title: Docs: add saturated session recovery guide

ambiguity.why_not_hard: Primary intent (docs about session/compaction recovery plus config reference updates) is clear; the only uncertainty is how many boundary labels (config/tests/reliability/runtime) to promote given the larger-than-docs file set.

per_label_rationale.docs: PR title and body center on adding a saturated-session recovery guide and updating compaction/configuration reference docs; multiple docs/*.md files added/updated.
per_label_rationale.sessions: Core subject is session saturation, recovery order, session-reset recommendation, and session-management-compaction reference.
per_label_rationale.memory: Touches memoryFlush behavior, durable memory storage before compaction, and memory-flush help copy.
per_label_rationale.config: Extensive config-layer changes: configuration-reference, defaults.ts, schema.help, zod-schema agent-defaults, and compaction settings/guard field docs.

excluded_label_rationale.tests_ci: Many test files are touched, but they support the compaction/config changes rather than being the central CI/test focus.
excluded_label_rationale.reliability: Recovery and guard themes are present, but this is a docs/config-facing change, not a concrete retry/crash recovery fix.
excluded_label_rationale.agent_runtime: Compaction guard runtime files appear, but the PR frames itself as docs/operator guidance, not runtime orchestration changes.

title: ACP parent session stuck until refresh when yielded waiting for child completion

ambiguity.why_not_hard: Core ownership (acp, sessions, reliability) is clear; only the runtime vs queueing boundary for the wake/event path is debatable.

per_label_rationale.acp: Issue is explicitly about ACP parent/child sessions and the ACP parent-stream-relay (src/acp/parent-stream-relay.ts), with sessions_spawn runtime:acp and ACP session resumption.
per_label_rationale.sessions: Centers on parent/child session state, yield/resume, persisting yield-wait state, and session wedged until refresh — core session identity/lifecycle behavior.
per_label_rationale.reliability: Describes a wedged/stuck non-responsive session, state divergence, and stabilization patch to recover via heartbeat wake path — classic reliability fix.
per_label_rationale.agent_runtime: Involves heartbeat wake scheduler, child task completion, requestHeartbeatNow, and runtime lifecycle state tracking (yieldWait) for parent/child orchestration.

excluded_label_rationale.queueing: enqueueSystemEvent and completion follow-ups touch event ordering, but the central problem is session resume/wake state rather than queue/lane/backpressure mechanics; plausible boundary label.
excluded_label_rationale.notifications: Comments mention 'no notification fires,' but this refers to internal system events, not outbound notification delivery policy.
excluded_label_rationale.ui_tui: Manual UI refresh is only a symptom; the fix is in the ACP relay/runtime, not the UI surface.

title: feat: Add opt-in ACP parent completion notify for sessions_spawn

ambiguity.why_not_hard: Core ownership (ACP/sessions/notifications/runtime) is clear; only the api_surface boundary and runtime-vs-notifications emphasis create mild uncertainty.

per_label_rationale.acp: Feature targets ACP `mode:"run"` spawns and ACP-specific completion routing for `sessions_spawn`; touches src/agents/acp-spawn*.ts.
per_label_rationale.sessions: Centers on `sessions_spawn`, parent/child session keys, session store loading, and routing completions back to the parent session.
per_label_rationale.notifications: Core purpose is fixing channel-facing completion delivery — routing terminal completion through the announce/notify flow instead of system events.
per_label_rationale.agent_runtime: Reuses subagent announce flow, terminal phase handling, and child task lifecycle completion routing.

excluded_label_rationale.api_surface: Only a 2-line change to gateway/protocol/schema/agent.ts and a new tool param; not the central contract change, so excluded as boundary.
excluded_label_rationale.coding_agents: Codex appears in test fixtures as an ACP agent example but the PR is about internal parent completion routing, not external coding-agent integration.
excluded_label_rationale.acpx: Body notes ACP/ACPX execution flow is unchanged; no ACPX-specific transport/proxy work.

title: browser tool: add force parameter for click and expose evaluate action

ambiguity.why_not_hard: Primary routing (browser_automation) is unambiguous; only the secondary security/api framing is debatable.

per_label_rationale.browser_automation: Central: proposes adding a force click parameter and an evaluate action to the Playwright-based browser tool, including hit-test/visibility/click simulation behavior.
per_label_rationale.security: The evaluate action exposes arbitrary JavaScript execution as an escape hatch, and the gateway already blocks the javascript: navigation vector — a deliberate unsafe-access prevention concern.

excluded_label_rationale.api_surface: Boundary: the gateway routing/tool-action contract is involved, but the change is fundamentally about browser tool capabilities rather than a general HTTP/API surface contract.
excluded_label_rationale.exec_tools: The browser tool has action params/schemas, but this is browser-specific interaction, not shell/command exec tooling.

ambiguity.why_not_hard: The core trio (skills_plugins, auth_identity, security) is clearly supported by the diff and description; only api_surface is a genuinely debatable boundary label.

per_label_rationale.skills_plugins: Core change adds a public plugin SDK registration API (registerProviderRuntimeAuthOverride) wired through src/plugins/* registry, api-builder, types, and runtime; this is a plugin product surface.
per_label_rationale.auth_identity: Provides runtime auth/credential resolution for model providers (anthropic/openai/openrouter), with auth modes api-key/oauth/token/aws-sdk and resolveApiKeyForProvider integration.
per_label_rationale.security: Explicitly about brokered, short-lived, vault-backed credentials and labeled auth-provider/security-boundary; reentrancy guard, error logging, and defense-in-depth validation are central.

excluded_label_rationale.api_surface: Despite being titled 'API', this is a plugin SDK/TypeScript contract, not an HTTP/gateway/REST/SSE endpoint; api_surface targets HTTP request/response contracts, so it is a boundary false-positive here.
excluded_label_rationale.local_model_providers: Targets built-in hosted cloud providers (anthropic/openai/openrouter), not local or user-configured OpenAI-compatible local backends, so this provider-auth layer does not route here.
excluded_label_rationale.gateway: Gateway test helpers are touched only incidentally to register the new plugin API; gateway behavior is not central.

title: ACP startup identity reconcile warns on terminal one-shot sessions

ambiguity.why_not_hard: Core ownership (acp/acpx/gateway/sessions) is clear from explicit cues; only reliability is a genuine boundary candidate.

per_label_rationale.acp: Issue centers on ACP startup identity reconciliation, ACP sessions, and ACP runtime via sessions_spawn(runtime="acp").
per_label_rationale.acpx: ACP backend is explicitly acpx; records carry acpxRecordId/acpxSessionId and direct plugin-local acpx one-shot prompts are tested.
per_label_rationale.gateway: The warning occurs during gateway startup reconcile; gateway startup behavior is the surface of the bug.
per_label_rationale.sessions: Concerns persisted session identity, terminal one-shot session records, session stores, and identity.state pending/closed handling.

excluded_label_rationale.reliability: Stale terminal metadata and non-fatal cleanup framing is plausible, but the fix is specifically session-identity reconcile logic rather than general crash/retry recovery.
excluded_label_rationale.agent_runtime: ACP runtime is mentioned, but the issue is about session identity persistence/reconcile, not runner/harness lifecycle.

title: feat(plugins): expose ACP spawn and prompt in plugin runtime

ambiguity.why_not_hard: Core ownership (plugins runtime + ACP + config + delivery) is clear; ambiguity is only over a couple of boundary labels like api_surface/gateway.

per_label_rationale.skills_plugins: Core change exposes new api.runtime.acp.spawn/prompt in the plugin runtime/SDK (src/plugins/runtime/*, plugin-runtime-mock), a plugin product surface.
per_label_rationale.acp: Adds ACP-backed agent session dispatch via spawnAcpDirect and ACP follow-up prompts (acp-spawn, runtime-acp.runtime.ts).
per_label_rationale.config: Introduces plugins.allowAcpSpawn opt-in flag in types.plugins.ts and zod-schema.ts with schema/help/labels updates.
per_label_rationale.notifications: Central motivation is channel delivery of ACP output (deliver:true to Discord/Telegram), closing the delivery gap that runTurn() leaves.

excluded_label_rationale.api_surface: The api.runtime namespace is plugin SDK runtime API, not an HTTP/REST/SSE contract; it belongs under skills_plugins rather than api_surface despite the current canonical label.
excluded_label_rationale.gateway: callGateway is used as a delivery vehicle but the PR is about plugin runtime exposure, not gateway behavior/startup/routes.
excluded_label_rationale.chat_integrations: Discord/Telegram are referenced only as delivery targets; no specific platform integration code changes.
excluded_label_rationale.agent_runtime: Subagent/ACP dispatch is referenced, but orchestration runtime itself is not the changed subject—plugin-facing exposure is.

title: Event loop saturation during startup: synchronous model-prewarm and session-locks block event loop for 28-64 seconds

ambiguity.why_not_hard: Core ownership (reliability/gateway/sessions startup blocking) is clear; only the model-prewarm sidecar's label home is genuinely ambiguous.

per_label_rationale.reliability: Core issue is event loop saturation, 28-64s blocking, heap pressure, crash-loop, and gateway restart cascade — classic reliability failure during startup.
per_label_rationale.gateway: Failures center on openclaw-gateway.service startup, gateway ready timing, and systemd restart cascade re-triggering saturation.
per_label_rationale.sessions: session-locks sidecar and 168 session stores (~3.7MB JSON) parsed synchronously on every startup are a named blocking culprit.
per_label_rationale.chat_integrations: Primary user-visible failure is Discord WS READY never firing and heartbeat ACK loss closing the connection.

excluded_label_rationale.model_serving: Model-prewarm sidecar is mentioned as a blocking culprit, but this is startup model warming/lifecycle blocking the event loop, not endpoint serving/streaming protocol behavior; weakly plausible boundary.
excluded_label_rationale.local_models: 'Prewarm'/cold-start cues could suggest local model warmup, but no local backend (Ollama/GGUF/local GPU) is named, so not central.
excluded_label_rationale.packaging_deployment: systemd service and RestartSec appear, but only as the restart mechanism, not as a packaging/deployment change.
